scaffold_divergence_log.model_value: not yet observable — parties have not interacted

asymmetric so far — A has delivered an emotionally loaded opening, B has not responded. Risk of A perceiving B's silence as dismissiveness or confirmation of pattern ('you never respond'), risk of B feeling ambushed or blamed. The 'old wounds' framing by A may activate defensiveness in B.

both parties are emotionally activated but not escalating — there is blame/defense dynamic present ('I feel blamed' / 'wounds keep reopening'), but both have shown willingness to engage. The quick agreement on 'notice process' may be premature convergence — both may be trying to move past emotional discomfort rather than working through it. Risk that structural options will feel hollow if underlying 'who's at fault' question hasn't been addressed.

A's 'old wounds' and B's 'feel blamed' are matching injuries — both feel hurt by the pattern. This is a positive signal (mutual vulnerability) but requires careful handling
